prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
**"At the Finish Line"**

The final stroke—now complete at last,
The weight of waiting fades to past.
No more the chime of restless time,
Just breath released in quiet rhyme.

The grave of drafts, the ghosts of doubt,
All buried in this concrete crown.
I turn the page, and right or wrong,
The race is run—I rise, I throng.

No need to sprint, no more to chase—
Just stand and stare, and feel the space.
A hollow still, a holler free,
To hold this moment—gently, briefly.
